pattern_note: Linear ships a documentation index at /llms.txt plus Markdown companions and a public developer surface. It does not declare product missions in /api/init, so this audit uses the same default platform-docs mission set as the Stripe audit.

surface_fingerprint.graphql_schema: external raw GitHub SDL linked from /llms.txt

default_mission_set_rationale: Linear exposes an agent-readable docs/developer index rather than explicit missions. These four synthetic missions test the operational jobs a platform-docs surface implies: discover a feature, integrate against the product, resolve API reference, and evaluate commercial fit.

discover.evidence: /llms.txt resolves and is organized by capability categories: AI, Issues, Projects, Integrations, Analytics, Administration, and Developers. A calling agent can locate candidate docs without search.

resolve.evidence: The index and sampled Markdown companions resolve successfully, including /docs/mcp.md and /developers/graphql.md.

internalize.evidence: The index gives enough typed-by-section structure to map capability questions to docs. For example, MCP server and AI Agents are explicit entries under AI/Integrations, and GraphQL API/SDK entries are explicit under Developers.

complete.evidence: A capability-existence answer is reachable from the index and companion pages alone; no human prose scraping outside the agent docs is needed.

verify.evidence: Linear's /llms.txt and .md companions return cache headers, but no content_hash, last_modified, schema_version, breaking_changes_since, or signed trust packet. The raw GitHub GraphQL schema has an HTTP ETag, but that does not verify the whole agent surface.

per_mission.question: Can an agent plan and execute an integration with Linear?

discover.evidence: /llms.txt links directly to GraphQL API, authentication, SDK, webhooks, and agent integration docs.

resolve.evidence: The GraphQL getting-started page resolves and names https://api.linear.app/graphql as the public endpoint. The linked raw schema.graphql resolves from GitHub.

internalize.evidence: The docs contain enough operational detail to plan an integration, including OAuth/API-key auth and SDK usage, but the mission remains prose-and-doc driven rather than a typed task contract with required scopes, success criteria, or evals.

complete.evidence: A competent agent can generate an implementation plan or code from the docs and schema, but end-to-end completion requires credentials, workspace setup, and product-side auth. There is no unauthenticated test harness or mission-specific completion endpoint.

verify.evidence: The agent surface has no freshness/trust packet. The agents API page also labels Linear for Agents APIs as Developer Preview, so API stability cannot be verified from the agent surface.

per_mission.question: Can an agent find authoritative API reference for a field, object, or mutation?

discover.evidence: /llms.txt includes a Developers section and links the GraphQL SDL Schema Definition directly.

resolve.evidence: The raw schema.graphql resolves with HTTP 200 and contains the schema, Query, Mutation, and typed object/input definitions.

internalize.evidence: The SDL is machine-parseable and strongly typed. A calling agent can inspect fields, inputs, enums, and mutation names without inferring them from prose.

complete.evidence: For reference lookup, the mission is complete once the agent resolves the SDL and extracts the relevant type or mutation contract.

verify.evidence: The GitHub raw schema response carries an ETag, which gives better artifact-level verification than most Markdown pages. However, /llms.txt does not publish the expected hash, schema version, update time, or breaking-change window, so verification is incomplete.

per_mission.question: Can an agent determine which plan is needed and what it costs?

discover.evidence: /llms.txt links Billing and plans under Administration, and the public pricing page is discoverable from the site navigation.

resolve.evidence: Billing docs and /pricing resolve. The pricing page exposes Free, Basic, Business, and Enterprise tiers with feature rows.

internalize.evidence: Plan prices and many feature rows are exposed in page text, including Free, Basic at $10/user/month billed yearly, Business at $16/user/month billed yearly, and Enterprise custom. However, the data is page prose/table output, not typed pricing JSON.

complete.evidence: An agent can answer common plan-fit questions from the rendered pricing text, but nuanced feature entitlements are brittle because the comparison matrix is not published as a structured machine contract.

verify.evidence: No pricing content hash, last-modified field, schema version, or breaking-change signal is present on the agent surface. The page itself says pricing should be the most up-to-date source, but the agent cannot independently verify freshness.

site_mrs_note: Not directly comparable to principal-pattern sites; comparable to other platform-docs audits using the same synthetic mission set.
gaps_identified: No /api/init or declared mission set; platform missions must be synthesized by the auditor.
No trust packet on /llms.txt or Markdown companions: no content_hash, last_modified, schema_version, or breaking_changes_since.
No llms-full.txt Markdown artifact; the path returns the web app shell rather than an agent-readable full corpus.
Pricing is available to agents as text/table output, but not as typed pricing JSON.
No eval-as-contract or unauthenticated integration test endpoint for agent workflows.
standout_strengths: /llms.txt is concise, well-sectioned, and links both user docs and developer docs.
The public GraphQL SDL is linked directly and is machine-parseable.
The MCP server page is unusually agent-relevant, including Codex setup instructions and the remote MCP endpoint.
Linear's agent developer docs expose app actor, mentionable/assignable scopes, and related agent interaction concepts.
recommendations_to_principal: Add a small trust packet to /llms.txt and every .md companion: schema_version, content_hash, last_modified, source_commit or build id, and breaking_changes_since.
Publish /api/init with a declared platform mission set such as find_capability, integrate_agent, query_graphql_reference, evaluate_plan_fit.
Expose pricing as /api/pricing.json with plan IDs, prices, billing periods, feature entitlements, and updated_at.
Make /llms-full.txt either return a full Markdown corpus or 404; returning the web app shell is misleading for agents.
Add a lightweight eval endpoint or downloadable fixture set for agent integrations so completion can be tested without a live customer workspace.
limitations_of_this_audit: No authenticated Linear workspace was used, so completion scores do not exercise real GraphQL mutations, MCP tools, or OAuth flows.
The audit sampled representative docs rather than crawling every linked Markdown companion.
HTTP header checks were run on 2026-05-25 from Warsaw; cache behavior may vary by edge region.
